steps.title: Spot the true subject
steps.content: The blank verb's subject is not the idea but rather the various cultural and ideological frameworks a person has internalized—a plural noun phrase.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: subject-verb agreement. The true subject is the plural noun phrase "the various cultural and ideological frameworks a person has internalized" — not the singular "the idea." Rule: the verb must agree with its head noun, regardless of intervening phrases.
  • "underlies": Singular verb. Agrees with "the idea" (a distractor noun), not the true plural subject "frameworks." ✗
  • "is underlying": Singular progressive — same number error as A, and the progressive tense is unneeded for a general truth. ✗
  • "underlie": Plural base-form verb. Correctly agrees with the plural subject "frameworks." ✓
  • "has been underlying": Singular perfect progressive — wrong in both number and aspect. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
